title: arrabidaea agnus-castus
intro: Arrabidaea agnus-castus, also known as the chaste tree, is a flowering plant native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional European and Middle Eastern herbalism. The plant's roots and leaves contain a compound called agnuside, which has been shown to have hormonal-balancing properties and has been used to treat menstrual irregularities, menopausal symptoms, and hormonal imbalances. Herbal preparations of the chaste tree include teas, capsules, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of PMS, hot flashes, and mood swings.
